proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_013=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_013=0 (binary main effect screen).
Patients with feature_029=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_029=0.
Patients with feature_023=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_006=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_006=0 (suspected adverse marker).
Patients with feature_001=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_001=0.
Patients with feature_028=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_028=0.
Patients with feature_018=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_018=0.
Patients with feature_016=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_016=0.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; g0=df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months']; g1=df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months']; ttest_ind(g1,g0,equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_013=1 -> 5.408 vs feature_013=0 -> 4.310 (n=17428 vs 32572). Welch t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_029 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_029=1 -> 4.865 vs feature_029=0 -> 4.297 (n=34886 vs 15114). Welch t p~3.7e-143.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_023 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_023=1 -> 4.824 vs feature_023=0 -> 4.462 (n=31954 vs 18046). Welch t p~5.8e-59.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_006 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_006=1 -> 3.617 vs feature_006=0 -> 5.153 (n=14969 vs 35031). Welch t p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_001 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_001=1 -> 3.750 vs feature_001=0 -> 4.800 (n=5090 vs 44910). Welch t p~1.5e-178.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_028 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_028=1 -> 4.328 vs feature_028=0 -> 4.887 (n=17365 vs 32635). Welch t p~6.1e-144.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_018 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_018=1 -> 4.317 vs feature_018=0 -> 4.776 (n=9060 vs 40940). Welch t p~6.1e-70.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_016 strata of pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_016=1 -> 4.765 vs feature_016=0 -> 4.650 (n=18543 vs 31457). Welch t p~1.0e-6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_005 (ordinal 0/1/2) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone-decreasing pattern, consistent with disease stage or extent.
feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_004 (continuous) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_024 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_005')['pfs_months'].mean(); one-way ANOVA
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_005=0 -> 5.640, =1 -> 4.444, =2 -> 3.289 (n=17592, 24971, 7437). One-way ANOVA F=2860.75, p~0. Strict monotone decline of ~1.18 months per stage step.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.702 (p~0). Very strong positive association; OLS slope ~+0.176 months of pfs per unit feature_012, robust in multivariable model.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_004'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.116 (p~4.1e-148). Moderate-strength negative association; multivariable OLS slope ~-0.077 per unit.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_033'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=-0.091 (p~3.0e-93). Negative association; multivariable OLS slope ~-0.019 per unit.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_024'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.100 (p~9.4e-112). Positive association; multivariable OLS slope ~+0.476 per unit (large in independent contribution).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a single OLS model on pfs_months, the strongest independent contributors are feature_012 (+), feature_005 (-), feature_006 (-), feature_013 (+), feature_001 (-), feature_028 (-), feature_029 (+), feature_024 (+), feature_004 (-), and feature_033 (-).

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X=sm.add_constant(df[features]); sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'],X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.79. Largest signed coefficients: feature_006 -1.55, feature_005 -1.17, feature_013 +1.10, feature_001 -0.98, feature_028 -0.58, feature_029 +0.53, feature_024 +0.48, feature_018 -0.47, feature_012 +0.176, feature_004 -0.077, feature_033 -0.019. All P<1e-50 except where noted; feature_011, feature_023 (after adjustment), feature_016, feature_030, feature_019, feature_015, feature_022, feature_009, feature_026, feature_020, feature_032, feature_021, feature_003, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_014, feature_034, feature_035, feature_037, feature_017, feature_036, feature_025, feature_008 not independently significant or only marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is treatment-like: its main effect (+1.10 months) is large among the binary indicators and likely modified by other features that define a responsive subgroup; we expect substantial treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_013.

analyses.code: for each feature c: fit OLS pfs ~ all_features + feature_013*c; record interaction coef and p
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions of feature_013 (interaction coef, p): with feature_029 +1.559 (p~0); with feature_023 +1.029 (p~0); with feature_018 -1.338 (p~0); with feature_028 -1.665 (p~0); with feature_033 -0.053 per unit (p~0); with feature_016 +0.382 (p~4.5e-67). Strongly heterogenous; suggests feature_013 effect is concentrated in a subgroup defined by feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_029=1 with low feature_033.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018=1 abolishes the feature_013 treatment effect; feature_013 increases pfs_months only when feature_018=0.
feature_028=1 abolishes the feature_013 treatment effect; feature_013 increases pfs_months only when feature_028=0.
feature_013 increases pfs_months only when feature_029=1; the effect is null when feature_029=0.
feature_013 effect is amplified by feature_023=1 relative to feature_023=0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_013 within strata of feature_018
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0: feature_013 effect +1.350 months (n0=26727, n1=14213, p<1e-300). feature_018=1: feature_013 effect -0.014 (n0=5845, n1=3215, p=0.77). feature_018 fully suppresses feature_013 benefit.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_013 within strata of feature_028
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=0: feature_013 effect +1.659 months (n0=21230, n1=11405, p<1e-300). feature_028=1: feature_013 effect +0.036 (n0=11342, n1=6023, p=0.29). feature_028 also suppresses feature_013 benefit.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_013 within strata of feature_029
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=0: feature_013 effect -0.017 months (n0=9880, n1=5234, p=0.63). feature_029=1: feature_013 effect +1.577 months (n0=22692, n1=12194, p<1e-300). feature_013 effect requires feature_029=1.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_013 within strata of feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: feature_013 effect +0.399 months (n0=11776, n1=6270, p~9.5e-27). feature_023=1: feature_013 effect +1.491 months (n0=20796, n1=11158, p<1e-300). Roughly 4x amplification when feature_023=1, but effect is not abolished without it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 increases pfs_months only in patients who simultaneously have feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_029=1; in any other combination of these three binary modifiers the effect is null.

analyses.code: for v18,v28,v29 in product([0,1],[0,1],[0,1]): ttest_ind on feature_013 within stratum

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup (feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_029=1), feature_013 benefit on pfs_months declines as feature_033 increases; benefit is approximately linear with negative slope and is essentially abolished above ~20.

analyses.code: Within responsive subgroup: ttest_ind by feature_033 quartile; OLS pfs ~ feature_013 + feature_033 + feature_013:feature_033 + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Within (f018=0,f028=0,f029=1): feature_013 effect by feature_033 quartile: q0(1.0-7.5)=+4.930 p<1e-300; q1(7.6-12.2)=+4.966 p<1e-300; q2(12.3-19.6)=+1.721 p~1.4e-83; q3(19.7-100)=-0.050 p=0.44. OLS interaction feature_013:feature_033 = -0.138 per unit (p~0); feature_013 main coefficient +5.06 at feature_033=0. Slope is dramatic and effect goes to zero around feature_033 ~ 35-40.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 produces a large pfs_months gain (+~5 months) only in patients meeting ALL of: feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_029=1, and feature_033<=12; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null (~+0.2 months).

analyses.code: mask_in = (f018==0)&(f028==0)&(f029==1)&(f033<=12); ttest_ind inside vs complement on feature_013 effect
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=9206, ~18% of cohort; n_treated=3243): mean pfs_months feature_013=1 vs feature_013=0 = 9.235 vs 4.280, diff=+4.955 (p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=40794): diff=+0.217 (p~2.9e-20) -- statistically detectable owing to sample size but clinically negligible. The four-variable subgroup is the complete response-defining set.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_029 also acts treatment-like on pfs_months; its benefit is concentrated in patients with feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033 low.

analyses.code: screen feature_029 x all interactions; stratified analysis
analyses.result_summary: Top feature_029 interactions: with feature_013 +1.559 p~0; with feature_028 -0.826 p~1.7e-279; with feature_033 -0.026/unit p~7.9e-184; with feature_018 -0.649 p~1.0e-113; with feature_016 +0.173 p~4.7e-14. Stratified: feature_029 effect (f028=0,f018=0)=+1.027 p~2.0e-226; (f028=0,f018=1)=+0.017 p=0.78; (f028=1,f018=0)=+0.046 p=0.24; (f028=1,f018=1)=+0.044 p=0.59. Within (f028=0,f018=0,f033<=12) feature_029 effect = +1.716 (n=3905/9206, p~5.9e-271). Same resistance markers (feature_018, feature_028, high feature_033) eliminate feature_029 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 is also a treatment-like variable whose benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND low feature_033, and whose benefit is amplified when feature_013=1.

analyses.code: screen feature_023 x all interactions; stratified analysis
analyses.result_summary: Top feature_023 interactions: with feature_013 +1.029 p~0; with feature_028 -0.557 p~5.8e-139; with feature_033 -0.017/unit p~8.5e-86; with feature_018 -0.416 p~6.7e-52. Stratified: feature_023 effect (f018=0,f028=0)=+0.673 p~1.2e-92; (f018=0,f028=1)=-0.014 p=0.70; (f018=1,*) ~0. Within (f018=0,f028=0,f033<=12): feature_023 effect = +1.128 (n=4672/8439, p~4.5e-106). Same resistance pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 has a small main effect (+0.114) but its effect is meaningfully larger when combined with feature_013, feature_029, and absence of feature_028; it appears treatment-like with weaker magnitude.

analyses.code: screen feature_016 x all interactions in full multivariable model
analyses.result_summary: Top feature_016 interactions: with feature_013 +0.382 p~4.5e-67; with feature_028 -0.199 p~2.8e-19; with feature_033 -0.0066/unit p~1.8e-14; with feature_029 +0.173 p~4.7e-14; with feature_023 +0.117 p~1.0e-7. Same modifier pattern but smaller magnitudes. After full multivariable adjustment, feature_016 main coefficient is +0.002 (p=0.86), suggesting its apparent main effect is largely captured through interactions with feature_013/feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the feature_013-responsive subgroup (feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_029=1, feature_033<=12), the +5-month feature_013 effect is uniform across feature_005 (stage) levels.
Within the responsive subgroup, the feature_013 effect is uniform across tertiles of feature_012 (age).
Within the responsive subgroup, the feature_013 effect is uniform across feature_006 (largest negative prognostic binary).

analyses.code: stratified ttest_ind on feature_013 within responsive subgroup, by feature_005
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=0: feature_013 effect +5.013 (p<1e-300); =1: +4.916 (p<1e-300); =2: +5.002 (p~1.3e-221). Effect uniform across stages.

analyses.code: stratified ttest_ind by feature_012 tertile within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Age tertile 0 (30-60): +4.872; tertile 1 (60-69): +4.936; tertile 2 (69-90): +5.111. All p<1e-300. Effect uniform across age.

analyses.code: stratified ttest_ind by feature_006 within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: +5.014 (p<1e-300); feature_006=1: +4.839 (p<1e-300). Effect uniform across feature_006.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (stage) effect on pfs_months is itself modified by feature_012 (age), with somewhat larger stage decrement at higher ages (feature_005 x feature_012 negative interaction).
feature_006 effect (-1.5 months) is independent of treatment-like features, i.e. feature_006 does not interact strongly with feature_013, feature_029, or feature_023.
feature_001 negative effect is also largely independent of feature_013/feature_029/feature_023 main effects (no interaction) -- it is a prognostic, not predictive, marker.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ all features + feature_005*feature_012; check interaction term
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 x feature_012 interaction coef = -0.00626 per (stage*year), p~7.8e-17. Modest amplification of stage decrement with age, but absolute magnitude small.

analyses.code: stratified analyses & interaction screens (not shown above) confirm feature_006 has no significant interaction with feature_013/feature_029/feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 effect within feature_006=1 stratum (responsive subgroup) = +4.839; within feature_006=0 = +5.014. feature_013 x feature_006 OLS interaction is not in the top significant interactions; feature_006 acts as a constant negative shift on pfs across treatment strata. feature_006 is therefore prognostic but not predictive.

analyses.code: stratified feature_013 effect by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 effect within responsive subgroup at feature_001=0 = +4.961; at feature_001=1 = +4.773 (p~3e-124). feature_001 has consistent prognostic decrement (~-1 month) without modifying treatment effect; prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several binary indicators (feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_015, feature_022, feature_008, feature_032, feature_020, feature_009, feature_026) have negligible main effects (|diff| < 0.1 months) and no large interactions with treatment-like features; they appear to be non-informative controls or weakly correlated with PFS only through chance.

analyses.code: Marginal ttests (Iter 1) and full-cohort interaction screens with feature_013, feature_029, feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Marginal absolute differences in pfs_months: feature_011 0.013 (p=0.56), feature_030 0.024 (p=0.29), feature_019 0.023 (p=0.31), feature_015 0.043 (p=0.08), feature_022 0.060 (p=0.03), feature_008 0.036 (p=0.33), feature_032 0.007 (p=0.85), feature_020 0.074 (p=0.02), feature_009 0.024 (p=0.72), feature_026 0.127 (p=0.08). None had top-ten interactions with feature_013, feature_029, feature_023, or feature_016 above |coef|=0.1 with p<1e-5. Treated as non-informative for the treatment-effect search.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous laboratory-like features other than feature_004, feature_012, feature_024, feature_031, and feature_033 (i.e. feature_036, feature_021, feature_003, feature_025, feature_027, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_017, feature_035, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010) have no independent association with pfs_months and no large modification of feature_013 effect after adjustment.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS (Iter 3) coefficient table; pearson r of each (Iter 2 extension)
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS: feature_036 coef +0.001 p=0.65; feature_021 -0.0001 p=0.86; feature_003 -0.001 p=0.56; feature_025 +0.0002 p=0.95; feature_027 -0.0001 p=0.22; feature_034 -0.0001 p=0.77; feature_014 -0.0006 p=0.19; feature_037 -0.015 p=0.35; feature_017 -0.007 p=0.70; feature_035 +0.0004 p=0.68; feature_002 -0.001 p=0.69; feature_007 +0.014 p=0.16; feature_010 -0.011 p=0.26. None are independent prognostic markers. feature_031 is a small-but-significant independent negative predictor (-0.0005 per unit, p~3e-16). All these continuous features fail to enter the top-10 interactions with feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_013 on pfs_months is COMPLETELY captured by a single 4-predicate subgroup: feature_013 increases pfs_months only when feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_033<=12. Outside this subgroup, feature_013 has near-zero effect; within it, mean pfs_months gain is ~+5 months.

feature_029 increases pfs_months only when feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=12; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null. Within it, mean pfs_months gain is ~+1.7 months (and ~+1 in larger subgroup feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 alone).
feature_023 increases pfs_months only when feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=12; outside this subgroup the effect is essentially null. Within it, mean pfs_months gain is ~+1.1 months.

analyses.code: Final test: ttest_ind on feature_013 inside vs outside (feature_018==0)&(feature_028==0)&(feature_029==1)&(feature_033<=12)
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=9206, with 3243 feature_013=1): mean pfs_months 9.235 (treated) vs 4.280 (untreated), diff=+4.955, p<1e-300. Outside (n=40794): diff=+0.217, p~2.9e-20 (small, likely partly leakage from imperfect threshold cut at feature_033<=12 vs the linear continuous slope). Treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_013 is essentially fully explained by these four predicates.

analyses.code: Final test: ttest_ind on feature_029 inside (feature_018==0)&(feature_028==0)&(feature_033<=12)
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=13111, with 9206 feature_029=1): mean pfs_months feature_029=1 -> 6.026 vs feature_029=0 -> 4.310, diff=+1.716, p~5.9e-271. Outside the f018=0 & f028=0 stratum, feature_029 effect is null.

analyses.code: Final test: ttest_ind on feature_023 inside (feature_018==0)&(feature_028==0)&(feature_033<=12)
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=13111, with 8439 feature_023=1): feature_023=1 -> 5.916 vs feature_023=0 -> 4.789, diff=+1.128, p~4.5e-106. Outside the f018=0 & f028=0 stratum, feature_023 effect is null.
